satire_attempt: Local CTO Smiles Slightly Before Speaking, Single-Handedly Resolving Eight-Week Migration Debate. Sources confirm the smile was 'not amused but settled,' a phrase the management training company plans to license for the upcoming 'Krishna Smile Workshop' ($2,400/seat). Industry observers note this is the same body language that has historically resolved Mahabharata-scale conflicts.
verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the verse's explicit refusal of the read: 'A reader extracts a leadership tactic from this verse — always smile before delivering hard feedback — they have over-read it.' The counter-example explicitly disqualifies the workshop satire: 'A leader who performs a smile to manipulate the room is not enacting Krishna in 2.10; they are performing affect.' To mock the verse you have to disagree with the meta-claim that affect-without-state is inversion, which is a real argument. PASS.
